Content Matters Podcast: The Time is Right: Implementing Structure Through Content Delivery with Frank Miller
description: Frank Miller joined the podcast to talk about a delivery-first approach to structured content and explained why Content Delivery Platforms (CDPs) are a practical gateway to structured content adoption.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Truth Collapse: When AIs are Participants in Culture with Noz Urbina
description: Noz Urbina, Founder & Omnichannel Strategist, Urbina Consulting, joins us on the Content Matters podcast to explain what Truth Collapse means, how it’s present in our society today, and the boundaries we need around AI technologies and their impact.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Cathy McKnight on Content Operation in 2025
description: Cathy McKnight, Chief Problem Solver at The Content Advisory, joined us for a deep dive conversation on all things content operations in 2025 - strategy, tech, and even AI.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Alan Porter on Developing Persona-based Content: The Good, the Bad, and the Ugly
description: Alan Porter, Founder of Content Pool, joined us on the Podcast to explain how to build personas that can help us deliver the best content-driven customer experiences.

title: Content Matters Podcast: AI-Powered Content Strategies with Lance Cummings
description: Lance Cummings joined us on the podcast to talk about how we can best leverage AI tools and strategies for improved content operations and writing.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Customer Outcome Focused Storytelling with Alan Porter
description: Alan Porter, Founder of Content Pool, joined us on the Podcast to help us understand how we can use storytelling structures to create better technical content for our customers.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Rebecca Schneider on Taxonomy, Metadata, and AI
description: If you think you don’t need taxonomy, metadata, and structured content now that you have generative AI, think again. Rebecca Schneider from AvenueCX explains in the episode of the podcast.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Tyler Lessard on Building A Media Brand
description: Tyler Lessard, founder of Outside-In Marketing, helps us understand what’s required to build a media brand and why it’s so important for companies today.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Decode Customer Desires: Mastering Content Connection with Christine Crandell
description: How well do you know your customers? Christine Crandell explains how we can decode our customer desires for a better customer experience.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Joe Gollner on Content 5.0: Are We Ready For It?
description: Joe Gollner of Gnostyx Research joined us on the Content Matters podcast to fill us in on what Content 4.0 is and how it impacts content strategy.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Managing Product Information with Matt Reiner
description: Matt Reiner is a Content Creator for K15t, a company that provides solutions for Atlassian. He has worked on multiple product teams in a range of roles including technical writer, product marketer, content designer, and speaker. Matt joined us on the podcast to talk about the challenges and opportunities related to creating and managing product information.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Mark Kilens On People-First Go-to-Market
description: Mark Kilens, CEO and Co-founder of TACK, a media network and go-to-market fir, joined us on the podcast to talk about his GTM model, why content marketing is ripe for disruption, what he learned during his time at HubSpot and Drift, and why community and the right content are so important for business success today.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Pablo Gonzalez On Category Design
description: We learned all about category design on the Content Matters Podcast from Pablo Gonzalez, co-founder of the Category Thinkers community.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Viral Bajaria on Generative AI in RevOps
description: 6sense co-founder and CTO Viral Bajaria joined the Content Matter Podcast to talk about generative AI, large language models and how they are using in it the Revenue AI platform.

title: Content Matters Podcast: PR, Content and Comms Strategy with Kristin Hege
description: Kristin Hege is an experienced public relations professional who joined us on the Content Matters podcast to talk about the evolving public relations industry.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Stopping "Random Acts of Marketing" with Chris Carr
description: Chris Carr, founder and CEO of marketing agency Farotech shares insights and advice to marketers trying to succeed today.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Cathy McKnight On The People, Process, and Tech of Content Operations
description: Cathy McKnight, Chief Problem Solver at The Content Advisory, joined the podcast to talk about content strategy and content operations.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Scott Brinker On Marketing Platforms Ecosystems
description:
The amount of change happening in marketing today is mind boggling and getting increasingly difficult to keep up with. But continue to try and in this episode, Scott Brinker, Chief Marketing Technologist and the VP of Platform Ecosystem at HubSpot, brings his unique and deeply experienced view of what’s happening.  

We talked about platforms, martech stacks, low-code, and (of course) generative AI. We also chatted about his work at HubSpot and his book, Hacking Marketing (agile software development).

title: Content Matters Podcast: A Conversation on AI with Paul Roetzer
description: Paul Roetzer, CEO and Founder of the Marketing AI Insititute joined us on the podcast to talk about what's happening with generative AI, the opportunities and challenges, and what we need to be thinking about.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Sangram Vajre on Rethinking the Path to Go-to-Market
description:
Marketing is continually evolving, and the strategies B2B marketers use are many: demand gen, lead gen, account-based marketing, product-led growth, etc. How do you know what is the right go-to-market approach? Sangram Vajre, co-founder and CEO of GTM Partners, joined us to talk about go-to-market and how to design the right strategy for your organization. We also took a step back in time and talked about how Sangram got into marketing and why he loves it.

You will hear:

How Sangram “fell” into marketing
His time at Terminus and the marketing approach that built the ABM category
Why he decided to write the MOVE book.
Why he started GTM Partners and what the company is trying to help tech vendors and organizations achieve with their go-to-market strategies.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Brian Piper on Content, SEO, Analytics, Web3, Oh My!
description: Brian Piper, Director of Content Strategy and Assessment, University of Rochester, joined the podcast to talk about content marketing, analytics, SEO, Web3 and more.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Building a Social Media Marketing Strategy with Alex Lee
description: Alex Lee, the Partner Success Manager for Diginomica joined us on the Content Matters Podcast to talk about how to build a strong social media marketing strategy.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Andrew Sevillia on Customer Advocacy
description: Andrew Sevillia is a 15-year veteran of customer advocacy and customer marketing. He joined us on the Content Matters podcast to talk about why and how companies can create great customer advocacy programs.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Moni Oloyede on Marketing Operations and Content Strategy
description: We often think of marketing operations and content strategy are distinct elements of a marketing strategy performed by different teams. But there is a relationship between the two and you can’t be good in one without a solid understanding of the other. Moni Oloyede, Director of Marketing Infrastructure at Fidelis Cybersecurity and a digital marketing expert and speaker, joined us on the Content Matters podcast to talk about the interrelationship between these two areas of marketing and shared some guidance on how you can bring them together to create successful customer experiences.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Jennifer Allen on Being a Chief Evangelist
description: Jennifer Allen has taken on the role of Chief Evangelist in her company, Challenger. It’s new role, one that she worked hard to achieve. She joined us on the podcast to talk about the role, what it involves and how it helps a company build a strong presence. She also shared her experiences working in Sales and talked about how the role needs to evolve to better support customers, including acting more like a marketer than a traditional salesperson.

title: Content Matters Podcast: Hilary Marsh on Creating and Operationalizing Content Strategy
description:
There is so much to understand about content strategy, and Hilary Marsh, Chief Strategist of The Content Company joined us on the Content Matters podcast to talk about some areas that are not as widely discussed. Our focus for our talk was more on content strategy for intranets and for companies where content is their primary offering (like associations and non-profits), but you could apply much of what Hilary shared to all content strategy work. Key topics included how content strategy has evolved, what organizations are doing well and not so well and much more, including:

- How to think about content as an investment.
- How content strategy for an intranet is different from content strategy for a website (or isn’t).
- Why content strategy is an HR issue and needs to be a part of everyone’s work.
- How to operationalize content strategy.
- How content management technology supports content strategy.
- The audience engagement journey (you have to listen to learn what this is).

title: Content Matters Podcast: Chantelle Nash on Improving the Employee Digital Experience
description: We talk a lot about customer experience, but the employee experience is equally important in being successful. Chantelle is the Learning Systems and Innovation Leader at Baker Hughes. She joined us on the Content Matters Podcast to discuss why it’s important to consider employee experiences as one element of a company-wide perspective on digital experience.

title: Content Matters Podcast: The Personalization Paradox with Guest Val Swisher
description:
We are fortunate to kick off our fourth season with Val Swisher, Founder and CEO of Content Rules, a consulting firm that delivers content strategy, development, transformation, and optimization services. Val joined us to talk about something every company wants to do – provide personalized experiences at scale.

She’s authored a book on the topic - The Personalization Paradox: Why Companies Fail (and How to Succeed) at Delivering Personalized Experiences at Scale with Regina Lynn Preciado, a senior content strategist with Content Rules.

We talked about the need for content standardization to enable personalization at scale and what organizations need to do from a content strategy and technology perspective.

Here’s what you’ll learn:

How Val defines personalization
What is the Personalization Paradox?
What is content standardization and how you achieve it? What needs to be standardized?
What are the biggest challenges to providing personalized content (at scale)?
How should a company think about personalizing content in a global context?

title: Welcome to Ingeniux CMS: Video Tour
Ingeniux CMS 10.5 Release
description: This webinar provides a look back at what was in the previous release of Ingeniux CMS (CMS 10.3), details on the recently released CMS 10.5, and a sneak peek at what you can expect to find in 10.6.

title: Content Matters Webinar: Content Engineering: Designing an Intelligent Content Solution
description:
Webinar by Caliper and Ingeniux.

Ingeniux Content Beyond Boundaries.
